explanation: This command uses the -R flag to set up a reverse SSH tunnel mapping port 3389 (RDP) on the remote host to localhost. It matches the Sigma rule by including ':3389' and '-R' in the command line.
This command uses the -L flag to set up a local SSH tunnel mapping port 3389 (RDP) on the local machine to localhost. It matches the Sigma rule by including ':3389' and '-L' in the command line.
This command uses the -P flag to specify the SSH port and the -R flag to set up a reverse SSH tunnel for port 3389 (RDP). It matches the Sigma rule by including ':3389' and '-R' in the command line.
